title: Macro Matters: Huw Worthington on ECB, Gilts and Rate Outlook

title: Odd Lots: How the 1994 World Cup Transformed Football (Podcast)
summary: The last time the World Cup came to the US was 1994. Before then, the World Cup was an enormously popular event with surprisingly limited commercial significance; the 1990 tournament in Italy, for instance, lost money for broadcasters. title: Iraq Seeks Higher Oil Allowance as OPEC Membership Rankles
summary: Iraq stepped up its push for bigger OPEC production quotas as it seeks to recoup oil sales that were lost because of the Iran war, even raising the prospect that it could consider an exit in the future.

title: Braskem Seeks Emergency Protection as Debt Talks Hit Snags
summary: Brazilian petrochemical giant Braskem SA filed for emergency protection against creditors as talks with for an out-of-court solution to its mounting debt woes hit snags.

title: Major Saudi Oil Terminal to Restart as Gulf Reboot Ramps Up
summary: Top oil exporter Saudi Arabia is set to restart loading crude at the giant Ras Tanura terminal inside the Persian Gulf, a landmark moment in the resumption of Middle Eastern supplies after the Iran war.

title: Wall Street Sees AI Backlash as Rising Risk to Tech-Fueled Rally
summary: Market professionals already on edge about the staying power of soaring artificial intelligence stocks are starting to grapple with another risk: public anger toward the technology.

title: H&M’s Sales Miss Estimates as Turnaround Questions Persist
summary: H&M AB reported disappointing sales, posing a fresh challenge to the Swedish fast-fashion retailer’s turnaround efforts amid intense competition and cautious consumer spending.

title: US Premarket Movers for June 25, 2026
summary: S&P 500 Index futures climb 0.7% as of 7:42 a.m. in New York. Stocks are set to rally as a forecast from Micron reinvigorates the AI trade.

title: Stocks Rally as Micron Buoys AI Trade Ahead of PCE: Markets Wrap
summary: Stocks rallied across the world after Micron Technology Inc.’s blowout forecast reaffirmed the bull case for the artificial-intelligence trade. Sentiment drew further support from oil prices returning to prewar levels.

title: Bolsonaro Family Tension Rises as Michelle Criticizes Flávio
summary: Former First Lady Michelle Bolsonaro signaled she plans to remain on the sidelines of Flávio Bolsonaro’s presidential campaign after what she described as a disrespectful phone call with her stepson, exposing fresh tensions within the family at the center of Brazil’s right-wing opposition to President Luiz Inácio Lula da Silva.

title: Brazil’s Police Launches Second Phase of Probe Into Americanas
summary: Brazil’s Federal Police and federal prosecutors launched the second phase of a probe that previously targeted Brazilian retailer Americanas SA, deepening an investigation into alleged accounting fraud, according to a statement on Thursday.

title: Stocks Rally as Micron Lifts AI Optimism Ahead of PCE | Bloomberg Brief 6/25/2026
summary: US equity futures jump after Micron's forecast shattered expectations. Oil prices slide back to pre-war levels as investors look ahead to PCE numbers. Sanjeev Rana of CLSA breaks down Micron's earnings. Stephanie Guild of Robinhood discusses what strength in memory chips could mean for the broader market. (Source: Bloomberg)

title: BofA’s Raedler Says Defensives Ready to Roar if AI Trade Falters
summary: Investors should switch their focus to defensive sectors if the artificial intelligence trade starts to unravel, according to Sebastian Raedler, head of European equity strategy at Bank of America Corp.

title: Private Credit’s Big Arbitrage Trade Gains Backing From Advisers
summary: On paper, it seems like a no-brainer trade: Cash out of one private credit fund at 100% of net asset value and plow the money back into a similar vehicle that’s trading at a substantially discounted price.

title: Iraq Walks Back Threat on Leaving OPEC Over Output Limits
summary: Iraq isn’t considering leaving OPEC, the nation’s oil ministry said, just a few hours after saying that doing so was an eventual possibility.

title: Fannie, Freddie Boost Risk to Levels That Once Shook Wall Street
summary: Fannie Mae and Freddie Mac are taking on more interest-rate risk in their rapidly growing investment portfolios, driving a key gauge of their exposure to levels that rattled Wall Street two decades ago.

title: China Begins €5 Billion Bond Sale, Largest Ever in Euros
summary: China kicked off marketing up to €5 billion ($5.7 billion) of sovereign bonds in what could be its largest-ever such deal in euros.

title: ECB Rate-Hike Forecasts Pared Back as Oil Regains Pre-War Level
summary: A sharp drop in oil prices amid progress in Middle-East peace talks has prompted some economists to scale back expectations for European Central Bank interest-rate increases.

title: Engines and Elevators Lift German Deals Past $140 Billion
summary: Germany’s dealmakers are seeing some of their long-wished-for transactions come to fruition, driving the country to one of its busiest years for mergers and acquisitions in decades.

title: Brent Oil Erases Wartime Gains as Hormuz Reopening Boosts Supply
summary: Brent oil erased all of its wartime gains after flows through the Strait of Hormuz ramped up following progress on a US-Iran peace deal.

title: Micron Surges on AI Boom as Brent Oil Erases Iran War Gains | The Pulse 6/25/2026
summary:
"The Pulse With Francine Lacqua" is all about conversations with high profile guests in the beating heart of global business, economics, finance and politics. Based in London, we go wherever the story is, bringing you exclusive interviews and market-moving scoops.

Today's guests: Christian Keller, Barclays Head of Economics Research; John Kerry, Former US Secretary of State; Nils Schmid, German Deputy Defense Minister; Theo Baker, Author of 'How to Rule the World'  (Source: Bloomberg)

title: Gold Steadies Near $4,000 as Traders Weigh Interest-Rate Outlook
summary: Gold traded in a narrow range, a day after a resurgent US dollar and the prospect of higher interest rates pushed the metal below $4,000 an ounce for the first time since November.

title: Hungary Yields Approach UK Gilts as Pro-EU Turn Fuels Rally
summary: Hungary’s bonds have rallied so much that yields are approaching those in the UK, the latest sign that investors are backing the new government’s plans to fix the economy and join the euro region.

title: EasyJet Shows Willingness to Talk After Next Castlelake Bid
summary: EasyJet Plc signaled willingness to entertain a higher offer from Castlelake LP by granting the US investment firm more time and some access to its books in order to sweeten its latest bid, which values the UK discount carrier at about £4.93 billion ($6.5 billion)

title: Micron Delivers the AI Reassurance Wall Street Was Craving
summary: The US memory-chip maker released a quarterly sales forecast that crushed estimates, easing AI fears.

title: Gilt Returns Climb to Three-Month High After Oil-Fueled Recovery
summary: Returns on UK government bonds are the highest in three months as tumbling oil prices offset investor concerns about unpredictable politics.

title: AI IPO Wealth Seen Bolstering Luxury Industry Hurt by Iran War
summary: The luxury sector is set to grow more slowly than previously predicted because of the Iran war, but wealth creation from the SpaceX listing and future US share sales by OpenAI and Anthropic could cushion the blow for struggling brands selling pricey handbags and watches, according to Bain & Co.

title: Jamieson Wellness Explores Selling Itself, Taps BMO and Canaccord to Advise
summary: Jamieson Wellness Inc., the Canadian vitamins and supplements maker, is working with Bank of Montreal and Canaccord Genuity Group Inc. to explore a sale.

title: NLB Chief Touts 40% Premium, Heritage in Bidding War for Addiko
summary: After two years of trying to get a foothold in neighboring Croatia by buying Addiko Bank AG, Slovenia’s largest lender isn’t ready to give up.

title: Switzerland’s Capital Plan for UBS Wins Wholehearted IMF Backing
summary: The Swiss government’s plan to make UBS Group AG hold significantly more capital was praised wholeheartedly by the International Monetary Fund.

title: SK Hynix ADR Plans Leave Arb Traders Waiting on One Key Answer
summary: SK Hynix Inc.’s planned $29 billion US listing has arbitrage investors dissecting securities filings and peppering brokers with questions about a single unresolved issue: whether the Korean chipmaker’s American depository receipts can be freely exchanged for Seoul-listed shares, a feature that could determine whether price gaps persist between the two markets.

title: US Tech Stocks Set to Rally as Micron Outlook Fuels AI Optimism
summary: US stock futures jumped as Micron Technology Inc.’s solid forecast reinforced confidence about demand for artificial intelligence-related chips.
